rationale: This page contains a detailed infographic timeline of the Dutch East India Company (VOC) in India, covering its formation, objectives, key locations, trade items, major battles, and eventual exit. It is highly factual and suitable for generating multiple-choice questions for history exams.
cleanedText:
Dutch East India Company (VOC) in India
Timeline - Quick Revision (Prelims)
1602: VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie)
- First joint-stock company of the world.
- Objective: Break the Portuguese spice monopoly.
- Focus on trade (not territorial rule).
1605: Dutch Arrive in India
- First factory: Masulipatnam.
PULICAT: Dutch HQ in India (Coromandel Coast).
Trade Items:
- Spices
- Textiles (Coromandel, Bengal)
- Silk
Locations in India: Surat, Pulicat, Nagapattinam, Cochin.
1759: Battle of Bedara
- Dutch Defeated.
- English East India Co. defeats VOC.
- Ends Dutch ambitions in Bengal.
1824-25: Anglo-Dutch Treaty
- Dutch Finally EXIT.
- Dutch HAD: Pulicat (HQ in India), Nagapattinam, Chinsurah, Ceylon...
- Took WHOLE Indonesia via Treaty.
KEY FACTS!
- Dutch HAD: Pulicat (HQ in India), Nagapattinam, Chinsurah, Ceylon...
- Took WHOLE Indonesia via Treaty.
